items.headline: PLD Athena Phase B site work permitted at Rue Ferrari
items.summary: A $35 million site work permit issued for PLD Athena Phase B at 5853 Rue Ferrari. This is the largest construction project in this permit batch and suggests a major development underway in San José.

items.headline: Tous Les Jours bakery-café permitted on Evergreen Place
items.summary: Building permit issued for a new Tous Les Jours location at 2394 Evergreen Pl. The Korean bakery chain is expanding its Bay Area presence with this San José restaurant.

items.headline: Public art agreement for Seeds of Peace Monument at Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: San José City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust regarding the Seeds of Peace Monument, an artwork by artist Mario Chiodo, to be located at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The agreement governs installation and terms for the public artwork.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District budget for FY 2026-27
items.summary: City Council took action on the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District's FY 2026-27 budget report and annual assessment. The business improvement district uses assessments on property owners to fund area improvements and services.

items.headline: Cupertino updates Health and Safety Element of General Plan
items.summary: City Council reviewed updates to the Health and Safety Element of the Cupertino General Plan (2015–2040). The General Plan guides long-term land use, development, and policy priorities across the city.

items.headline: City Council appoints new permanent City Manager
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager on May 19. This fills the top administrative position overseeing city operations and staff.

items.headline: City releases draft water shortage plan for public input
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission reviewed Sunnyvale's Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. The contingency plan outlines how the city will respond if water supplies become constrained.

items.headline: Spring cycle community grants and events applications under review
items.summary: The City Council considered 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program applications from the spring cycle on May 18. Residents can apply for funding to support local events and neighborhood initiatives.
